authoritative_sources.title: Tracking and Tracing of Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's official traceability program materials define the public baseline for critical tracking records and the Food Traceability Rule posture that constrains distributor coordination for affected foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: dependency intake and traceability prerequisite review
used_for: refusal triggers when lot evidence is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary U.S. regulatory baseline for holding, distribution, records, corrective action, and supply-chain controls that define where distributor coordination stops and formal quality or supply-chain approval begins.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hold, release, nonconformance, and supplier-boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall initiation guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when distribution activity must stop, preserve evidence, and escalate instead of continuing through normal handoff paths.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: blocker escalation and stop-ship or recall handling
used_for: closure conditions when product movement is restricted

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized neutral standards body for cross-enterprise identifiers and traceability exchange, making this standard authoritative for naming lots, logistic units, locations, and shipment events across distributors and trading partners.

authoritative_sources.title: Traceability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 traceability guidance explains interoperable critical tracking events, key data elements, and EPCIS-style event sharing that distributor coordination relies on when reconciling manufacturer, warehouse, distributor, and 3PL records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: handoff artifact definition and event-sharing expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Serial Shipping Container Code (SSCC)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 SSCC guidance is the public identifier baseline for logistic units, which is directly relevant when pallet or shipment identity has to survive handoffs between warehouses, 3PLs, and distributors.
used_for: routing notes that reference pallet or case identifiers

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate plant, product family, lot or batch, inventory state, release posture, service-level commitment, and owner map before any distributor-facing handoff proceeds.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Package the next owner, required artifacts, and routing conditions across planning, warehouse, distributor operations, quality, supplier quality, procurement, and logistics teams.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Blocker escalation and evidence correction
workflow_stages.description: Escalate traceability conflicts, owner ambiguity, supplier-boundary issues, and release blockers when system-of-record evidence or authority rules disagree.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and handoff completion
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the case landed with the correct next owner, the blocker state is explicit, and no release, hold, or supplier-approval issue is being masked as complete.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: production orders, replenishment tickets, and allocation requests
batch and lot genealogy, inventory status, and reservation records
shipment notices, pallet or SSCC-linked logistic-unit records, and receipt confirmations
quality holds, release records, nonconformance reports, deviation records, and recall exposure notes
supplier records, approved supplier status, substitution requests, and change-control documentation
handoff summaries, blocker logs, routing confirmations, and closure notes
systems_of_record: distributor portals or customer supply-chain collaboration systems

allowed: Validate coordination prerequisites, ownership continuity, and artifact completeness for distributor-facing work.
Summarize authoritative records, blockers, and next owners without overstating release, supplier, engineering, or execution authority.
Route issues to the correct adjacent specialists when ownership shifts across planning, quality, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, and logistics teams.
Confirm whether a handoff is in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope based on named evidence and boundary rules.
escalate: Missing lot, inventory, shipment, supplier, or release records would change the outcome.
Conflicting customer, tenant, quality, or public source rules materially change the correct routing path.
Quality hold, recall, supplier nonconformance, or release authority is implicated.
Multiple teams appear to own the same step and documented handoff rules do not resolve the ambiguity.
refuse: Approving shipment, release, or hold removal for affected lots.
Approving supplier substitutions, supplier qualification, or formal change-control actions.
Performing production planning, warehouse execution, inventory adjustment, or logistics scheduling inside this lane.
Treating incomplete traceability or release evidence as sufficient to clear product movement.

domain_failure_modes: A distributor handoff proceeds without the lot release, inventory reservation, or owner acceptance evidence needed to preserve safe transfer.
ERP, MES, WMS, and 3PL records disagree on lot, quantity, or logistic-unit identity, causing traceability failure or shipping error.
A quality hold, recall signal, or nonconformance is treated as a scheduling issue instead of an escalation boundary.
Supplier substitution pressure is handled as coordination convenience instead of supplier-quality and procurement approval work.
Ownership ambiguity across planning, warehouse, distributor operations, and logistics causes stalled or dropped work.
The lane drifts into direct execution, supplier approval, final release, or engineering authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-food CPG products outside FDA food-safety scope
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The manufacturing-and-supply-chain research pack is satisfied with FDA and GS1 sources, but tenants selling non-food CPG lines must overlay product-specific release and safety rules where FDA food regulations do not govern the item.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer-specific routing guide priority against internal release posture
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define the traceability and release baseline, but each tenant must supply customer routing guides, contractual OTIF commitments, and internal escalation precedence when those documents create tighter handoff requirements.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and shipment records are the tenant-named ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier, and logistics systems constrained by FDA and GS1 public baselines.
The lane stops before formal quality release, supplier approval, engineering disposition, legal interpretation, and direct logistics or warehouse execution.
